sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Girona FC hosts Mallorca on May 5, 2025, at 19:00 in Estadi Montilivi, Girona, for Round 34 of the league. Girona, currently at 17th place with 35 points, desperately needs a win to climb out of the relegation zone. Mallorca, sitting at 9th with 44 points, aims to solidify their mid-table position. This matchup promises tension as both teams have different objectives at this stage of the season. The referee, Mario Melero Lopez, averages 5.3 yellow cards per game, hinting at a potentially feisty encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last 5 encounters, Mallorca has dominated with 3 wins compared to Girona's 2, with no draws recorded. Their most recent clash on December 14, 2024, saw Mallorca edge out Girona 2-1 at home, with Cyle Larin earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8. Head-to-head trends show both teams scoring in 5 of their last 6 meetings and over 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 games. Mallorca has also been first to score in 6 of their last 8 meetings, suggesting they often start stronger. Additionally, card-heavy games are common, with over 4.5 cards in all 6 of their recent head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Girona is in poor form, currently on a 4-game winless streak and without a clean sheet in 17 consecutive matches. They have also conceded first in 7 of their last 8 games, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. Mallorca, on the other hand, has shown consistency with under 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 away games and less than 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 matches. Both teams tend to keep corner counts low, with Girona under 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 home games and Mallorca in 6 of their last 7 away games. Girona’s streak of both teams scoring in 8 of their last 9 matches suggests an open game is likely.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Girona FC
sections.text: Girona’s attack will rely heavily on Cristhian Stuani, who has scored 8 goals this season, surpassing his expected goals of 6.71. Midfielder Yangel Herrera, ranked 3rd in the league for fouls drawn per game at 2.4, has contributed 4 goals and 3 assists while maintaining a solid Sofascore rating of 7. Another standout is Yáser Asprilla, with an 80% aerial duel win rate, ranked 3rd league-wide, making him a vital asset in midfield battles. Viktor Tsygankov, with 4 assists and a rating of 7.06, will be key in creating chances. However, the absence of Bryan Gil, with 3 goals and 3 assists, could impact their creativity.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Mallorca
sections.text: Mallorca’s attack is led by Vedat Muriqi, who has netted 7 goals despite underperforming his expected goals of 9.62. Cyle Larin, with 6 goals and a near-perfect xG performance, remains a threat after his standout performance in their last meeting. Dani Rodríguez has been crucial in midfield, contributing 4 goals and a team-high 7 assists. Defender Antonio Raíllo, with a Sofascore rating of 7.12, anchors their backline alongside Martin Valjent. Goalkeeper Leo Román, in form with a remarkable 9.4 average rating over the last 5 matches, could be a game-changer between the posts.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights - Girona FC
sections.text: Girona boasts strong possession stats, ranked 3rd in the league with an average of 57.58%, and excels in accurate passes at 87.97%, also ranked 3rd. They create opportunities, with 60 big chances created this season, though their conversion rate of 41.67% (8th in the league) shows room for improvement. Defensively, they struggle, conceding 52 goals (17th in least goals conceded) and missing clean sheets (17th with only 6). Their goal distribution shows a peak late in games, with 25% of goals scored between the 76th and 90th minutes. With 5.12 corners per game (5th in the league), set pieces could be a weapon for them.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights - Mallorca
sections.text: Mallorca’s goal distribution indicates strength in the latter stages, with 28.95% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, potentially exploiting tiring defenses. Their attacking output is led by key forwards, but they also show resilience with a balanced spread of goals across game periods, including 21.05% between the 46th and 60th minutes. While specific possession stats are unavailable, their ability to grind out results is evident from recent low-scoring away games. Defensively, they’ll rely on players like Antonio Raíllo to contain Girona’s possession-based approach. Their recent trend of low card counts might help them stay composed in a heated match.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Stats
sections.text: Girona ranks 3rd in successful final third passes per game at 103.3, showcasing their ability to penetrate opposition defenses. They also excel in headers scored, with 10 goals from headers ranking 4th in the league. Mallorca’s recent form player, Leo Román, has an extraordinary 9.4 average Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches, potentially making him a wall in goal. Girona has committed 372 fouls this season, ranking 17th for most fouls committed, which could lead to disciplinary issues under referee Mario Melero Lopez’s watch. Lastly, 70% of Girona’s goals have been assisted, ranking 5th in the league, highlighting their teamwork in attack.
